metadata.title: Why interleaved parallel tool logs can still be traced to the right agent
metadata.topic: Reconstructing which subagent made which call
metadata.purpose: The SDK stamps every subagent tool call with `parent_tool_use_id` equal to the `tool_use_id` of the spawning `Task`; a hook reads it, maps it to an agent identity, and regroups the flat stream into a per-agent tree.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.still_lanes: geo (DAG with edge labels), c2v (hook extracting parent_tool_use_id), raster (terminal with agent+tool)
metadata.exclusions: quantum-research specifics, how parent IDs are generated, Task spawn internals
metadata.visual_object: A DAG — Task node at top, child tool-call nodes below, edges labeled with the parent ID, colored by agent.
metadata.hook: A lead agent spawns four subagents that fire 30 tool calls in parallel; the terminal streams them interleaved — which WebSearch belonged to which researcher?

beats.narration_text: Lead spawns 3 Researchers (each calling WebSearch repeatedly) plus a Data Analyst running Bash; calls stream past with no obvious owner.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: A parent spawns N children whose calls arrive interleaved; with only a flat log, how do you rebuild which call belongs to which child?
A lead agent spawns four subagents that fire 30 tool calls in parallel; the terminal streams them interleaved — which WebSearch belonged to which researcher?

The SDK stamps every subagent tool call with `parent_tool_use_id` equal to the `tool_use_id` of the spawning `Task`; a hook reads it, maps it to an agent identity, and regroups the flat stream into a per-agent tree.
beats.visual: A DAG — Task node at top, child tool-call nodes below, edges labeled with the parent ID, colored by agent.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: `Task("research quantum")` = `task_42`; Researcher's WebSearch `ws_100` carries `parent=task_42`; hook logs "RESEARCHER-1 → WebSearch (parent task_42)"; Data-Analyst's Bash carries a different parent, so they separate cleanly.

The SDK stamps every subagent tool call with `parent_tool_use_id` equal to the `tool_use_id` of the spawning `Task`; a hook reads it, maps it to an agent identity, and regroups the flat stream into a per-agent tree.
